top_stories.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
top_stories.summary: A critical authentication-bypass vulnerability in cPanel has been disclosed, with multiple proof-of-concept exploits rapidly emerging afterward. A researcher claims zero-day exploitation activity has been occurring for at least a month prior to public disclosure, putting millions of cPanel-managed systems at risk.

top_stories.title: ConsentFix v3 attacks target Azure with automated OAuth abuse
top_stories.summary: A new attack technique called ConsentFix v3 has emerged on hacker forums, targeting Azure environments through automated OAuth abuse. The attack builds on previous ConsentFix methods by adding automation capabilities, increasing the potential scale of OAuth consent phishing attacks against Azure/Entra ID environments.

top_stories.title: Progress Patches Critical MOVEit Automation Bug Enabling Authentication Bypass
top_stories.summary: Progress Software has released security patches for MOVEit Automation addressing two vulnerabilities, including a critical authentication bypass flaw. The affected product is an enterprise managed file transfer solution, and the patch resolves a flaw that could allow attackers to circumvent authentication controls.

top_stories.title: Cybercrime Groups Using Vishing and SSO Abuse in Rapid SaaS Extortion Attacks
top_stories.summary: Two cybercrime groups, Cordial Spider and Snarky Spider, are conducting rapid SaaS extortion attacks that abuse SSO and use vishing (voice phishing) to compromise identity systems. The attacks operate almost entirely within SaaS environments, leveraging SSO abuse as a primary attack vector to steal data while leaving minimal forensic traces.

top_stories.title: Learning from the Vercel breach: Shadow AI & OAuth sprawl
top_stories.summary: The Vercel breach demonstrated how a compromised third-party OAuth integration can create a direct attack path into connected environments. The incident highlights risks associated with OAuth sprawl and shadow AI integrations, where unauthorized or poorly governed OAuth apps can lead to widespread impact across downstream customers.

news.title: Progress warns of critical MOVEit Automation auth bypass flaw
news.summary: Progress Software issued a warning about a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in MOVEit Automation, its enterprise managed file transfer application. The flaw allows attackers to bypass authentication controls, requiring immediate patching by affected customers.

news.title: Poisoned Ruby Gems and Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines for Credential Theft
news.summary: A software supply chain attack campaign using malicious Ruby gems and Go modules has been attributed to the GitHub account "BufferZoneCorp," targeting CI pipelines for credential theft and SSH persistence. The attack uses sleeper packages to deliver payloads that enable credential harvesting and GitHub Actions tampering.

news.title: IAM Roles Anywhere now enforces VPC endpoint policies for the CreateSession API
news.summary: AWS IAM Roles Anywhere now enforces VPC endpoint policies for the CreateSession API, allowing administrators to control access at the network level. This update enables organizations to configure VPC endpoint policies to explicitly allow or deny CreateSession API calls, adding a network-based access control layer to certificate-based IAM authentication.

news.title: IAM tools help Oracle Red Bull Racing keep pace with strict F1 regulations
news.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing implemented 1Password's identity technology to improve efficiency in its aerodynamics testing procedures, which must comply with strict F1 regulations. The deployment focused on identity management to streamline access controls within the racing team's technical operations.

news.title: Govern your bots carefully or chaos could ensue
news.summary: Gartner warns that the rapid growth of AI agents—projected to reach over 150,000 per Global Fortune 500 enterprise by 2028—requires proper governance to prevent security and operational chaos. The article focuses on identity and access governance challenges posed by non-human identities (AI agents/bots), a direct concern for IAM professionals managing machine identity sprawl.

news.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
news.summary: cPanel has released security updates to fix a critical authentication vulnerability affecting all supported versions of cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM). The flaw impacts multiple authentication paths and could allow attackers to gain unauthorized access to the control panel software.

news.title: Organizing Agents’ memory at scale: Namespace design patterns in AgentCore Memory
news.summary: AWS published a technical post about designing namespace hierarchies and retrieval patterns for AgentCore Memory, an AI agent memory service. The post includes coverage of AWS IAM-based access control as one component of the implementation guidance.

news.title: AWS Transfer Family Terraform module now supports Okta and Microsoft Entra ID integration examples
news.summary: AWS has updated its Transfer Family Terraform module to include end-to-end deployment examples integrating Okta and Microsoft Entra ID as custom identity providers for authentication and access control. The update enables enterprises using these IdPs to configure AWS Transfer Family endpoints with their existing identity infrastructure.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. The group carried out SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and stole tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency, with their attack methods notably including SIM swapping and social engineering to bypass MFA and authentication systems.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.

news.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
news.summary: The article examines how AI agents with broad access to systems, files, and online services are creating new security challenges for organizations. The piece addresses how these autonomous tools blur boundaries around trusted access, insider threat, and authorization — concepts relevant to IAM but not the primary focus.
